$comment: Dynasty owner-economy pack (prefix ec.): sponsor signings, repair bills, the brink, windfalls, development milestones, and the bankruptcy ending (docs/dev/dynasty-tycoon-economy.md section 8). Follows 010-core.json format exactly; read-side only - these voice display projections of the folded money ledger, never fold inputs. All amounts arrive pre-formatted; every non-guaranteed token stays inside optional segments.

1960s: Motor racing has always burned money; the trick is burning it slower than the prize fund refills.
The books are kept in pencil in this paddock, and the pencil works hard.
1990s: The commercial department has its own pit wall now, and its own lap charts.
Sponsorship is the second championship, and it never rains itself off.
2010s: The finance model updates in real time; the racing still settles it on Sundays.
Cash flow is the one telemetry channel every team watches.
default: A racing team is a company that happens to be measured in laps.
The ledger never takes a weekend off.
Money is lap time wearing a suit.

1960s: Teams have vanished from entry lists for less; the paddock remembers every one.
The bank manager does not care who held the apex.
1990s: Administrators do not do slow out-laps. When the money stops, everything stops.
The paddock has watched famous names fold mid-season; nobody is exempt.
2010s: The insolvency spreadsheet has no sentiment column.
Racing debt compounds faster than downforce.
default: An empty tank stops a car; an empty account stops a team.
Every deficit round is a lap closer to the wall.
The grid takes no prisoners and the ledger takes fewer.

1960s: The fabricators will work the nights; the invoices will work the mornings.
Sheet metal is cheap; finished race cars are not.
2010s: The parts list prices itself; the crash structure does not negotiate.
Carbon repairs are billed by the layer.
default: The garage fixes the car; the office absorbs the rest.
Development is bought in increments and proven in laps.

templates.headline: New backing lands at {team}[[?sponsor: — {sponsor} signs on]]
templates.deck: Commercial desk, season {season}: the livery gains a name

sections.lead: The commercial ledger at {team} gains a line[[?sponsor: : {sponsor} has signed]]. Signing money lands now; the per-race payments follow every start, and the bonuses follow the results.
sections.close: Backing is bought with results and repaid the same way.

templates.headline: The deal behind the decal[[?sponsor: — inside the {sponsor} agreement]]
templates.summary: A commercial feature on {team}'s newest sponsorship deal[[?sponsor: with {sponsor}]].

sections.lead: Sponsorships are courtships with invoices. {team} closed one[[?sponsor: with {sponsor}]] — and in this paddock a signature is worth more than a season of promises.
sections.impact: The money changes the arithmetic: entry fees stop stinging, the development queue moves, and a bad Sunday costs sleep instead of survival.

templates.headline: The bill arrives: {team} counts the cost[[?venue: of {venue}]]
templates.deck: Garage desk, season {season}: a heavy weekend for the fabricators
templates.summary: {team} faces a major repair bill[[?amount: of {amount}]][[?venue: after {venue}]].

sections.lead: Some weekends are paid for twice: once on the stopwatch, once at the parts bin. {team}'s garage has a rebuild on its hands[[?amount: — the invoice reads {amount}]][[?venue: after the damage done at {venue}]].
sections.close: The car will be ready. The ledger takes longer to straighten.

templates.headline: What a shunt really costs
templates.deck: Garage feature, season {season}: the anatomy of a rebuild
templates.summary: A feature on the price {team} pays to rebuild[[?amount: — {amount} this time]].

sections.lead: Accident damage is the sport's least negotiable invoice. At {team} the fabricators have started the long nights[[?venue: after {venue}]][[?amount: , and the office has logged {amount} against a column that had other plans]].
sections.reliability: Every rebuilt corner must be as true as the jig can make it; nobody refunds a chassis that drives crooked.

templates.headline: {team} on the financial brink
templates.deck: Paddock desk, season {season}: the grace is spent
templates.summary: {team}'s deficit has run its full grace[[?amount: — the balance reads {amount}]].

sections.lead: The paddock's quietest crisis is a red column. {team} has raced through its financial grace[[?amount: — the account stands at {amount}]] — and the arithmetic is now brutally simple: the next deficit weekend is the last.
sections.close: Prize money, a sponsor cheque, a saved repair bill — anything positive buys another Sunday.

templates.headline: Racing on empty: inside {team}'s survival weekend
templates.summary: A feature on {team} racing while the money runs out[[?amount: — {amount} on the books]].

sections.lead: There are two races at {team} now. One is on the circuit. The other is in a ledger[[?amount: showing {amount}]], and it does not wait for the flag.
sections.impact: Everything is triaged: development stops, the spares list shrinks, and every classified finish is measured first in prize money.

templates.headline: The cheque clears: a windfall season for {team}
templates.deck: Commercial desk, season {season}: the review pays out

sections.lead: Season reviews are usually about points. This one at {team} was about the other column[[?amount: — {amount} landed at the settlement]], the kind of money that turns next year from a hope into a plan.
sections.close: Windfalls are spent twice: once on the car, once on the confidence.

templates.headline: How the front pays: {team}'s season settlement
templates.summary: A feature on {team}'s front-running season money[[?amount: — {amount} banked]].

sections.lead: The sharp end of a championship is also its payroll. {team}'s season settled rich[[?amount: — {amount} across prize money and bonuses]] — the compounding reward for running where the cameras point.
sections.impact: Money is the sport's quietest lap time: it becomes development increments, retained staff, and the margin to survive a bad crash without flinching.

templates.headline: {team}'s development programme reaches its ceiling
templates.deck: Technical desk, season {season}: the increments are spent
templates.summary: {team}'s car development reached its cap[[?milestone: at stage {milestone}]].

sections.lead: The technical office at {team} has run out of drawings to buy: the season's development programme is at its ceiling[[?milestone: — stage {milestone}]]. Whatever pace was purchasable has been purchased; the rest belongs to the driver.
sections.close: Next season the regulations shuffle and the ladder resets. For now: maximum car.

templates.headline: Bought speed: the {team} upgrade story
templates.summary: A feature on {team} completing its development programme[[?milestone: — stage {milestone}]].

sections.lead: Development money is the most honest money in racing: it either shows on the stopwatch or it shouts at you from the ledger. {team} has spent its way to the top of the programme[[?milestone: — stage {milestone}]], increment by audited increment.
sections.stats: Each stage cost more than the last; the escalation is the sport's oldest pricing model.

templates.headline: {team} is bankrupt: the Dynasty ends
templates.deck: Breaking: the money is gone and the entry is withdrawn
templates.summary: {team} folded[[?amount: with the account at {amount}]] — the career ends.

sections.lead: It ends not with a chequered flag but with a ledger that will not balance. {team} is bankrupt[[?amount: — the final account reads {amount}]][[?venue: , the collapse sealed at {venue}]]. The entry is withdrawn; the garage goes quiet.
sections.impact: The cars, the drawings, the unpaid ambitions — all of it passes to the administrators. The championship continues without waiting.
sections.close: Every racing ledger ends at zero eventually. This one just arrived early.

templates.headline: The team that ran out of road
templates.deck: Retrospective: how the money ended before the racing did
templates.summary: A retrospective on the collapse of {team}[[?amount: — final balance {amount}]].

sections.lead: Race teams rarely die of one bill. They die of arithmetic — deficit Sundays stacking quietly until the grace runs out. So it went at {team}[[?amount: , the books closing at {amount}]].
sections.close: The results stand. The debts, for once, do not transfer to the record book.
